statement: This record remains negative evidence on build 26100: the repo did not produce enough supporting proof to promote it into a normal actionable surface.
gating_reason: The strongest current-build code route points to the removable-storage policy path, while the intended Session Manager I/O runtime ETW lane stayed no-hit.

links.title: Win25H2Clean 96-key phase-0 existence batch
links.summary: The 96-key existence batch confirmed AllowRemoteDASD exists on the clean baseline at the intended Session Manager I/O path with observed value 0.

links.title: Path-aware static probe for AllowRemoteDASD
links.summary: The path-aware static pass found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe value hit for AllowRemoteDASD, but the naturally resolved current-build Ghidra function opened the removable-storage policy path rather than the intended Session Manager I/O path.

links.title: Path-aware lightweight ETW follow-up for AllowRemoteDASD
links.summary: The tools-hardened path-aware ETW lane completed shell-safe with a real I/O burst and produced real ETL/CSV outputs, but still did not capture an exact runtime read or intended-path line for AllowRemoteDASD.

links.title: Historical collision review for AllowRemoteDASD
links.summary: Earlier triage had already flagged AllowRemoteDASD as a likely removable-storage-policy collision rather than clean Session Manager I/O coverage.
